text:
There once was a very friendly bear named Oscar. Oscar lived happily deep in the forest, munching on all sorts of delicious mushrooms he discovered.

One day, Oscar encountered a peculiar-looking old owl. The owl spoke with an odd lilt.

"You old thing! You travel a lot?"

Oscar chuckled nervously.

"Yes! You think so?"

"That makes me happy!" said the owl. "You're talking. You share thoughts."

"You could say that," Oscar agreed warmly.

The owl perched beside Oscar.

"You've heard lovely things I've thought?"

"Oh yes! I love tales!" Oscar said eagerly.

The owl regaled Oscar with stories so captivating that Oscar didn't notice time slipping away.

After many moons, Oscar returned home.

"This trip?" Oscar asked his wife.

"Just an owl.”

They chuckled.

That same year, Oscar began dreaming stories almost nightly. They were full of owls, squirrels, moonlit journeys. Oscar wrote them down.

Years passed. Oscar passed
